title: Tribunal Clears U.S. Renewable Diesel of Trade Concerns, Signaling Stable Supply and Competitive Market
content: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal found no evidence that U.S. renewable diesel imports are dumped or unfairly subsidized, halting both its inquiry and the CBSAâ€™s investigations. This decision keeps cross-border supplies tariff-free, bolsters market certainty, and lets domestic and U.S. producers compete on equal footing. It also reinforces Canadaâ€™s low-carbon fuel goals by ensuring uninterrupted access to competitively priced feedstocks under a balanced, evidence-based trade-remedy system.
author: Analysts Account
